reasoning: Providing a concise summary of the election results described in the input.
output:
Leith Walk by-election (two-seat ward using STV):
- SNP topped the poll with John Lewis Ritchie on 2,290 first-preference votes; elected at stage one with a 7.6% swing from Labour.
- Labour candidate Marion Donaldson received 1,623 votes; Greens candidate Susan Jane Rae had 1,381 votes. Donaldson was elected at stage 10 after preferences were distributed.
Turnout: 25.1%.

Midlothian West by-election:
- SNP candidate Kelly Parry won with 1,540 votes; Labour candidate Ian Miller had 945 votes.
- By-election triggered after Owen Thompson was elected as SNP MP for Midlothian; the SNP held the seat with a swing of 6.3% from Labour.
